description: Logic+, Vogue Gen2, and Evomax 2 ranges — combi and system boilers common across UK housing and light commercial.

faultCodes.title: No central heating flow
faultCodes.summary: Pump's running but there's no flow round the system.
quickChecks: Bleed the auto air vent on top of the pump
Try freeing the pump at the centre screw
Check both flow and return valves are fully open

faultCodes.title: Ignition lockout
faultCodes.summary: Boiler tried to light but won't fire up.
quickChecks: Check gas at the hob or another appliance
Clean and re-gap the ignition electrode
Drain the condensate trap
Measure gas pressure under load
Check the condensate pipe isn't frozen — pour warm water over it

faultCodes.title: Five unsuccessful ignition attempts
faultCodes.summary: Boiler tried to light 5 times and gave up.
likelyCauses: Same as L2 — gas supply, ignition, flame-sense, gas valve
quickChecks: Work through the no-ignition flowchart

faultCodes.title: False flame detection
faultCodes.summary: Boiler's seeing a flame when there shouldn't be one.
quickChecks: Dry and inspect the ignition lead and electrode
Soap-test the burner with the valve closed

faultCodes.title: Overheat stat tripped
faultCodes.summary: Overheat stat has tripped.
quickChecks: Check the pump's running and system is vented
Look at the heat exchanger for sludge
Test overheat stat continuity when cold

faultCodes.title: Low system pressure
faultCodes.summary: System pressure has dropped too low.
quickChecks: Top up to 1.2 bar cold
Walk the system looking for leaks
Check the PRV discharge pipe outside

faultCodes.title: Flame loss
faultCodes.summary: Flame lit OK but went out mid-run.
quickChecks: Measure gas pressure at full rate
Check the flue terminal outside
Clean the flame-sense electrode

faultCodes.title: Fan fault
faultCodes.summary: Fan isn't running at the right speed.
quickChecks: Listen to the fan on start-up
Check 230V supply and tacho wiring

faultCodes.title: Flow NTC fault
faultCodes.summary: Flow NTC reading is wrong — open, shorted, or out of range.
quickChecks: Re-seat the connector
Measure resistance against the NTC chart
Check the NTC connector for green corrosion — clean or swap it

faultCodes.title: Return NTC fault
faultCodes.summary: Return NTC reading is wrong — open, shorted, or out of range.
quickChecks: Re-seat the connector
Measure resistance against the NTC chart

faultCodes.title: Outside sensor fault
faultCodes.summary: Outside sensor's reading wrong.
quickChecks: Check the external sensor
Inspect the cable run for damage

faultCodes.title: Low mains voltage
faultCodes.summary: Mains voltage has dropped too low for the PCB.
likelyCauses: Shared circuit dropping voltage under load
quickChecks: Measure voltage at the fused spur
Check for a loose neutral or live connection

faultCodes.title: CH flow switch open
faultCodes.summary: Flow switch says there's no water flow but the pump's running.
quickChecks: Flush the flow switch if you can get to it
Make sure the pump's actually running

faultCodes.title: PCB fault
faultCodes.summary: PCB has an internal fault.
quickChecks: Power-cycle the boiler
Check the PCB for moisture or water marks

faultCodes.title: Communication fault
faultCodes.summary: Display and main PCB aren't talking to each other.
quickChecks: Re-seat the display ribbon cable
Try a known-good display

faultCodes.title: BCC / chip card fault
faultCodes.summary: BCC chip card is missing or can't be read.
likelyCauses: BCC not put back after a PCB swap
quickChecks: Re-seat the BCC
Clean the contacts
Check the BCC matches the boiler rating

faultCodes.title: Ignition failure (Evomax 2)
faultCodes.summary: Won't light — commercial lockout.
quickChecks: Check gas pressure under load
Clean the electrode and check the gap
Drain the condensate trap

faultCodes.title: Flame loss during operation (Evomax 2)
faultCodes.summary: Flame lit but keeps cutting out.
quickChecks: Check gas working pressure
Inspect the flue terminal
Clean the flame-sense electrode

faultCodes.title: Fan fault (Evomax 2)
faultCodes.summary: Fan speed isn't matching what the PCB wants.
quickChecks: Listen to the fan and give it a visual check
Check wiring and supply

faultCodes.title: No flame detected after ignition
faultCodes.summary: Flame lights briefly then drops out straight away.
likelyCauses: Dirty or badly positioned flame-sense electrode
Gas pressure dropping once it fires
Weak flame — air/gas mix is off
quickChecks: Clean the flame-sense electrode with fine emery cloth
Check gas pressure holds steady while running
Inspect electrode lead for cracks near the boot connector
Check gas rate against the data badge

faultCodes.title: Air/gas ratio wrong
faultCodes.summary: Air/gas mix is wrong — safety lockout.
likelyCauses: Flue blocked — birds' nest, leaves, or debris
Condensate trap backed up into the heat exchanger
Fan speed wrong — dodgy fan or speed sensor
Soot or debris in the venturi / mixing chamber
quickChecks: Check the flue terminal is clear outside
Clean the condensate trap and siphon
Do a combustion analysis — CO/CO₂ will confirm it
Inspect the venturi for soot buildup

faultCodes.title: Controls wiring fault
faultCodes.summary: Controls wiring or OpenTherm link's faulty. Boiler still fires but smart features won't work.
likelyCauses: Room stat wiring loose or disconnected
OpenTherm controller not set up right
Short circuit on the controls wiring
quickChecks: Check wiring at the boiler terminal strip
OpenTherm fitted? Check it's powered and talking
Bridge L-N on the room stat terminals — if it fires, fault's in the wiring
Check for loose connections on the terminal block

faultCodes.title: Hot water too hot
faultCodes.summary: Hot water's coming out too hot — safety lockout.
likelyCauses: DHW sensor reading wrong — check actual temp to be sure
Diverter stuck partly open to heating side
PCB not modulating down quick enough
quickChecks: Run a hot tap and measure the actual water temp
If water's not actually hot, suspect the DHW NTC — check its resistance
Check the diverter valve moves freely to full DHW
Descale the plate heat exchanger if over 5 years old in a hard water area

faultCodes.title: Pressure too low
faultCodes.summary: Pressure too low — boiler won't fire until you top it up.
likelyCauses: Slow leak — rad valve, pipework joint, etc.
Expansion vessel gone — check the charge
quickChecks: Top up to 1.0–1.5 bar via the filling loop
Look for drips at rad valves, joints, and connections
Check expansion vessel charge at the Schrader valve (~0.75 bar cold)
If it keeps dropping, do a proper leak search
